content:
If this is donations for Thunderbird https://www.thunderbird.net/donate/help/

Help on donations to Mozilla https://www.mozillafoundation.org/en/donate/help/


As for the viruses messages, are these as small popups on bottom right corner of Firefox Windows? if so then these may just be web push notifications from sites you have allowed to send you notifications. Instead of useful information, some may send you notifications of junk, spam, fake virus/trojan alerts etc. You can revoke these sites from sending you notifications. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/push-notifications-firefox#w_how-do-i-revoke-web-push-permissions-for-a-specific-site

content: I can't say I've encountered that, but if I did, I would guess that it's an HTML-formatted message and there's something the code that interfered with copying. The first thing I'd try would be to view the message in Plain Text mode (menu item <View | Message Body As>.
creator: rednoise

content: Go to TB menu > Account Settings > R. click the account on the left > click the Delete button upper R. corner.
creator: siffemoz

content:
Please describe in detail what, exactly, happens when you "can not log in" - quote any error messages you see verbatim. Screen shots will suffice. Can you view your email on your providers web mail interface? Are all the messages you tried to delete still there or are they gone?

When a program crashes unexpectedly it often continues to run invisibly in the background. There are various ways to kill those phantom processes, but the easiest is to simply reboot the computer. Have you done that? If so and it's still misbehaving there are other measures to take, but start with that one.
creator: rednoise

content: Go to TB menu > Account Settings > account > Server Settings and make note of the User Name. Then scroll down to Outgoing Server, where there should be an entry for each of the three accounts. Make sure each of the three accounts' User Names are the same as their respective Server Settings User Names.
creator: siffemoz

content: That is a message from Google, not from Thunderbird. Google is asking if it's OK for a third-party program (Thunderbird in this case) to access your Google account. The notifications will stop once you either Accept, or remove the Thunderbird account that is trying to access the Google account. In other words, it's under your control.
